mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months signals aggressive monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and boosts liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend effect independent of any threshold crossing.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rate from 1.58% toward near-zero in emergency actions around March 2020
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20d forward returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of FEDFUNDS decline show no significant positive mean return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE, currently at a historic low of 3.5%, rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals an emerging labor market deterioration that precedes broad earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of Feb 2020, pre-pandemic low
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE rose >0.3pp in 3 months, 20d forward SPX returns were positive, or rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and residential demand that is more predictive of equity weakness than either series alone, because it reflects both discretionary spending and durable-goods investment pulling back simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 529766M and HOUST at 1567K as of latest pre-lockdown readings
failure_condition: If joint negative RSAFS/HOUST 3-month changes fail to produce significantly more negative 60d returns than either signal alone (interaction term insignificant at p>0.10), or rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a pandemic-induced supply-shock regime where mandated shutdowns impair production capacity, a decline in INDPRO signals sustained impairment of corporate earnings capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. Outside this regime, INDPRO declines are less predictive because they reflect ordinary cyclical slowdown rather than abrupt capacity loss.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month during declared shutdown period
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.2 as of Jan 2020, prior to expected pandemic-driven declines
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines during the shutdown regime are not followed by significantly more negative 60d returns than INDPRO declines in non-shock regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% approaching the zero lower bound, markets interpret this as signaling exhaustion of conventional monetary policy tools and heightened crisis severity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premia spike.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.58% with emergency cuts underway toward near-zero in March 2020
regime_hint: monetary policy exhaustion at zero lower bound
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS crossing below 0.25% are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or rank IC < 0.02, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in CPIAUCSL (falling inflation, deflation risk) and RSAFS (falling retail sales) signals a deflationary demand spiral in which falling prices and falling spending reinforce each other, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts independently.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 259.05, RSAFS at 529766M as of latest pre-crisis readings
failure_condition: If the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and RSAFS declines does not add explanatory power over the additive single-series model (incremental rank IC < 0.02), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO reflects healthy industrial production and corporate output, supporting near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This is an always-on trend relationship without any activation threshold.
failure_condition: If rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month growth and 5d forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a recession-onset regime, a joint decline in PAYEMS and a rise in UNRATE together signal an accelerating and self-reinforcing labor market collapse (job losses feeding into rising unemployment) that markets underprice relative to single-indicator moves, producing sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE simultaneously rises
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152544K and UNRATE at 3.5% as of Feb 2020, prior to expected pandemic labor shock
failure_condition: If joint PAYEMS decline and UNRATE rise events do not produce significantly more negative 60d SPX returns than either signal alone (interaction coefficient insignificant at p>0.10), or rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, the hypothesis is rejected.
